_doc: Sources the book engages with — prior-art books, papers, posts, talks, and the academic PDFs staged for extraction. Each source has at most one primary author (use `coauthors` for additional). `engagement` describes the book's stance toward the source. `representedBy` (optional) links to a Note that materially holds the source (e.g. a conversation transcript that doubles as a Note). `file` (optional) is the staged PDF filename. `availability` is one of: `open` (cleared to commit — file lives in content/sources/), `restricted` (in-copyright — file lives in the gitignored sources-local/, never committed or published), `unverified` (committed but licence not yet confirmed), `external` (no file held — cited by reference only). `tags` is a controlled vocabulary for navigation: `prior-art` (a work the book is in dialogue with), `adversary` (prior art that actively challenges the book's thesis), `evidence` (a source that backs or pressure-tests Claims), `internal` (the author's own corpus), `model-origin` (the source of a model or framing the book adapts).

authors.label: Corey Petty
authors.summary: The author of this book; some of his own earlier posts are treated as primary sources.

authors.label: Neil Postman
authors.summary: Media-ecology scholar. Amusing Ourselves to Death.

authors.label: Hugo Mercier
authors.summary: Cognitive scientist. Argues for robust epistemic vigilance.

authors.label: Cailin O'Connor
authors.summary: Philosopher of science. Network models of belief propagation.

authors.label: James Owen Weatherall
authors.summary: Philosopher of science. Co-author with O'Connor.

authors.label: C. Thi Nguyen
authors.summary: Philosopher. Distinguished epistemic bubbles from echo chambers.

authors.label: Susan Blackmore
authors.summary: Developed memetics as a research program.

authors.label: Yuval Noah Harari
authors.summary: Author of Nexus; framing on myths/bureaucracy and three realities.

authors.label: Tim Urban
authors.summary: Wait But Why author. The tablespoon-of-weeks visualization.

authors.label: Douglas Hofstadter
authors.summary: Cognitive scientist; author of Gödel, Escher, Bach. Originated the frame/outer/inner three-layer message model.

sources.label: The Democratization Paradox
sources.author: corey-petty
sources.summary: Covers the barriers/volume angle: curation layer, cost-shifting, creation-cheap/review-expensive, inverted-U relationship.
sources.engagement: primary internal source the book draws from

sources.label: The Double-Edged Sword of Deep Expertise
sources.author: corey-petty
sources.summary: Working out cross-domain intuition: chunking and deep structural analogies as the cognitive substrate of expert transfer; the four 'curse of expertise' failure modes (paradigm lock-in, overconfidence in cross-application, perceptual filtering, conceptual rigidity); and the prescription for cultivating versatile expertise. Claude-assisted.
sources.engagement: primary internal source — supplies Chapter 9's bridge-node mechanism

sources.label: General Theme (conversation transcript)
sources.author: corey-petty
sources.summary: Conversation transcript where the book concept got laid out coherently for the first time. The conclusion of Part IV lives raw here.
sources.engagement: primary internal source the book draws from

sources.label: Amusing Ourselves to Death
sources.author: neil-postman
sources.summary: Argues that television as a medium degraded public discourse. The 'medium shapes message' thesis applied to a specific case.
sources.engagement: the book needs to know what it's adding past Postman
sources.file: Amusing Ourselves to Death Public Discourse in the Age of Show Business.pdf

sources.label: Not Born Yesterday
sources.author: hugo-mercier
sources.summary: Direct challenge to the 'people are easily manipulated by simple memes' thread. Humans have more robust epistemic vigilance than the easy-manipulation narrative implies.

sources.label: The Misinformation Age
sources.author: cailin-oconnor
sources.summary: Formal network models of belief propagation. What kills truth in networks: selection on credibility plus social pressure, not lossy hops.

sources.label: Echo Chambers and Epistemic Bubbles
sources.author: c-thi-nguyen
sources.summary: Distinguishes epistemic bubbles (missing info) from echo chambers (active discrediting of outside info). Small networks tend toward chambers.
sources.engagement: foundational for the rebuilt Part IV

sources.label: Understanding Media
sources.author: marshall-mcluhan
sources.summary: 'The medium is the message.' The canonical reference for how media shape what can pass through them.

sources.label: The Selfish Gene
sources.author: richard-dawkins
sources.summary: Coined the term 'meme.' The selection-side view's foundational text.
sources.engagement: the selection-side view already has a literature; use it

sources.label: The Meme Machine
sources.author: susan-blackmore
sources.summary: Developed memetics into a sustained research program. Mechanisms of cultural selection.
sources.engagement: the selection-side view already has a literature; use it

sources.label: Nexus
sources.author: yuval-noah-harari
sources.summary: Information networks bound by myths and bureaucracy. Three realities (objective / subjective / intersubjective). Truth/order axis. Naive view of information.
sources.engagement: already in play; key framing reference

sources.label: Your Life in Weeks
sources.author: tim-urban
sources.summary: Tablespoon-of-diamonds visualization of a human lifespan. The receiver-budget intuition.
sources.engagement: cited as the source of the tablespoon-of-weeks framing

sources.label: An epidemiological approach to model viral propagation of memes
sources.summary: Models memetic propagation using epidemiological dynamics. Formal apparatus for the transport-stage math.
sources.engagement: candidate evidentiary source for Chapters 5 and 5b

sources.label: Information Evolution in Social Media
sources.summary: Empirical work on how information morphs as it traverses social-media networks.
sources.engagement: candidate evidentiary source for the bridge-zone distortion case studies

sources.label: Memetics — a critique
sources.summary: Critical assessment of memetics as a research program. Where the analogy with genetic transmission breaks down.
sources.engagement: candidate pressure-tester for the selection-side framing

sources.label: Psychology of Virality
sources.summary: Empirical and theoretical work on what makes content go viral. Identity, emotion, in-group signaling.
sources.engagement: candidate evidentiary source for Chapters 5b and 7

sources.label: Gödel, Escher, Bach: An Eternal Golden Braid
sources.author: douglas-hofstadter
sources.summary: Chapter VI, 'The Location of Meaning', develops the three-layer message model — frame (this is a message), outer (how to decode it), inner (what it means) — to ask whether meaning is intrinsic to a message or supplied by the decoder.
sources.engagement: source of the three-layer message model the book adapts

sources.label: How to Ethically Build Public Good Infrastructure
sources.author: corey-petty
sources.summary: Devcon VI talk, October 2022. Applies Hofstadter's three-layer message model to infrastructure design — and makes the key move of identifying the outer message with the medium.
sources.engagement: primary internal source — first place the three-layer model was bound to the medium
